title: Replace dead tower-lsp Client with NotificationSink trait for server→frontend notifications
analysis:
The in-process LSP bridge (InProcessLsp) bypasses tower-lsp's LspService dispatch layer by calling LanguageServer trait methods directly on a cloned ReifyLanguageServer. This means the tower-lsp ServerState never transitions to Initialized, so Client.send_notification() silently drops every message. The 3 publish_diagnostics() calls in server.rs (did_open, did_change, did_close) are dead code in in-process mode.

The current workaround in main.rs lsp_request() manually polls diagnostics after didOpen/didChange and emits them via Tauri events. This is fragile and doesn't generalize to other server-initiated notifications.

The fix: introduce a NotificationSink trait that publish_diagnostics() calls go through instead of Client. For in-process/Tauri mode, implement TauriNotificationSink that emits Tauri events. For stdio/TCP mode, implement ClientSink that wraps the tower-lsp Client. For tests, use NoOpSink or a RecordingSink.

There are ~14 call sites of LspService::new(ReifyLanguageServer::new) that need updating (10 in server.rs tests, 2 in integration tests, 1 in bridge.rs, 1 in lib.rs). The approach adds a with_sink constructor first for backward compatibility, then migrates all callers.

Key complexity: constructor signature change propagates through LspService::new closure, InProcessLsp::new, LspBridge::new, and the Tauri main.rs setup. In main.rs, LspBridge is currently created before Tauri setup(), but TauriNotificationSink needs an AppHandle. Solution: move LspBridge creation into setup() where AppHandle is available.

prerequisites.description: Verify existing tests pass before any changes (cargo test -p reify-lsp and cargo test -p reify-gui --lib)

steps.description: Write test in server.rs: noop_sink_implements_notification_sink — construct NoOpSink, wrap in Arc<dyn NotificationSink>, call publish_diagnostics on it (should compile and not panic). This test will fail to compile because NotificationSink and NoOpSink don't exist yet.

Define NotificationSink trait and NoOpSink struct in server.rs. Trait has publish_diagnostics(&self, uri: Url, diagnostics: Vec<Diagnostic>, version: Option<i32>). NoOpSink implements with empty body. Both are pub. Make step-1 test pass.

Write test in server.rs: server_with_sink_initializes — create server via ReifyLanguageServer::with_sink(client, Arc::new(NoOpSink)), call initialize(), verify it returns same capabilities. Will fail because with_sink constructor doesn't exist.

Add sink: Arc<dyn NotificationSink> field to ReifyLanguageServer. Add with_sink(client, sink) constructor. Update existing new(client) to delegate to with_sink(client, Arc::new(NoOpSink)) for backward compatibility. Derive(Clone) still works since Arc<dyn NotificationSink> is Clone. Make step-3 test pass; all existing tests remain green.

Write test in server.rs: sink_receives_diagnostics_on_did_open — define a RecordingSink (Arc<Mutex<Vec<(Url, Vec<Diagnostic>, Option<i32>)>>>) in the test module. Create server with RecordingSink via with_sink. Call did_open with bracket source. Assert that RecordingSink recorded exactly one publish_diagnostics call with the correct URI. This test will FAIL because publish_diagnostics still goes through self.client, not self.sink.

Replace all 3 self.client.publish_diagnostics(uri, diagnostics, version).await calls in server.rs with self.sink.publish_diagnostics(uri, diagnostics, version) (sync, no .await). Call sites: did_open (line 122-124), did_change (line 171-173), did_close (line 187-189). Make step-5 test pass.

Write test in server.rs: sink_receives_diagnostics_on_did_change — use RecordingSink, open a document, then did_change to broken source. Assert RecordingSink received 2 calls (one from did_open, one from did_change). Verify the second call contains error diagnostics.
Make step-7 test pass (should already pass after step-6 implementation; this step verifies and handles any edge cases).
Write test in server.rs: sink_receives_clear_on_did_close — use RecordingSink, open then close a document. Assert the last sink call has empty diagnostics vec (the clear-on-close notification).
Make step-9 test pass (should already pass after step-6; this step verifies the did_close path).

Write test in bridge.rs (or server.rs integration): in_process_lsp_with_sink_receives_diagnostics — create InProcessLsp::with_sink(Arc::new(RecordingSink)) and call handle_request('textDocument/didOpen', ...). Assert RecordingSink received diagnostics. Will fail because InProcessLsp::with_sink doesn't exist yet.

Add InProcessLsp::with_sink(sink: Arc<dyn NotificationSink>) constructor to bridge.rs. It passes the sink into LspService::new(|client| ReifyLanguageServer::with_sink(client, sink.clone())). Update InProcessLsp::new() to use NoOpSink. Export RecordingSink from server.rs for test use (cfg(test)). Make step-11 pass.

Write test in gui/src-tauri/src/tests/lsp_bridge_tests.rs: lsp_bridge_with_sink_routes_diagnostics — create LspBridge::with_sink(Arc::new(NoOpSink)), run a didOpen request, verify get_diagnostics returns results. Will fail because LspBridge::with_sink doesn't exist.

Add LspBridge::with_sink(sink: Arc<dyn NotificationSink>) to gui/src-tauri/src/lsp_bridge.rs. It passes sink to InProcessLsp::with_sink(). Update LspBridge::new() to use NoOpSink (backward compat). Make step-13 pass.

Add ClientSink in server.rs: wraps tower-lsp Client, implements NotificationSink by cloning Client and spawning tokio::spawn for the async publish_diagnostics call. Update lib.rs run_server() to use ClientSink: LspService::new(|client| { let sink = Arc::new(ClientSink::new(client.clone())); ReifyLanguageServer::with_sink(client, sink) }). Update integration tests (lifecycle.rs, interactive_edit_loop.rs) to use NoOpSink pattern.

Define TauriNotificationSink in main.rs: holds tauri::AppHandle, implements NotificationSink by calling app.emit('diagnostics', json!({uri, diagnostics})). Create it in setup() from app.handle().clone(). Move LspBridge creation into setup(): let sink = Arc::new(TauriNotificationSink { app: app.handle().clone() }); let bridge = LspBridge::with_sink(sink); app.manage(bridge). Remove the manual diagnostics polling workaround from lsp_request command (the extract_document_uri helper and the if-let-Some(uri) block that re-emits diagnostics).

Final cleanup: Rename with_sink to new and old new to new_default (or remove if all callers migrated). Update all remaining LspService::new(ReifyLanguageServer::new) patterns in server.rs tests to use a test helper fn: fn test_service() -> (LspService<ReifyLanguageServer>, tower_lsp::ClientSocket) { LspService::new(|c| ReifyLanguageServer::new(c, Arc::new(NoOpSink))) }. This reduces boilerplate across 10 test call sites. Verify all tests pass.

design_decisions.decision: NotificationSink trait methods are synchronous (not async)
design_decisions.rationale: Tauri's app.emit() is sync. For the Client wrapper (ClientSink), we use tokio::spawn to fire-and-forget the async call. This avoids async trait complexity and makes the trait usable in both sync and async contexts.

design_decisions.decision: Keep backward-compatible new(client) constructor during migration, add with_sink(client, sink)
design_decisions.rationale: Allows incremental migration of ~14 call sites without a big-bang change. Tests can continue using the old constructor until the final cleanup step. The backward-compat new() defaults to NoOpSink, which preserves existing behavior.

design_decisions.decision: Move LspBridge creation into Tauri setup() callback
design_decisions.rationale: TauriNotificationSink needs an AppHandle, which is only available during/after setup(). Currently LspBridge::new() is called before .setup(). Moving it into setup() via app.manage() is the standard Tauri pattern for state that depends on the app handle.

design_decisions.decision: Remove manual diagnostics polling workaround from lsp_request command
design_decisions.rationale: With TauriNotificationSink, diagnostics are emitted by the server during did_open/did_change processing. The manual polling in lsp_request would cause duplicate events. Removing it simplifies the code and eliminates the need for extract_document_uri.

design_decisions.decision: ClientSink uses tokio::spawn for async Client.publish_diagnostics()
design_decisions.rationale: NotificationSink methods are sync, but Client.publish_diagnostics() is async. tokio::spawn is fire-and-forget, which matches the expected behavior — the server doesn't wait for the notification to be sent. Client is Clone+Send+'static so this is safe.

design_decisions.decision: RecordingSink defined in #[cfg(test)] module for verification
design_decisions.rationale: Needed to assert that publish_diagnostics calls flow through the sink with correct arguments. Uses Arc<Mutex<Vec<...>>> to capture calls across async boundaries. Exported pub(crate) for use in bridge integration tests.

design_decisions.decision: NotificationSink trait lives in server.rs alongside ReifyLanguageServer
design_decisions.rationale: The trait is tightly coupled to the server's notification mechanism. Keeping it in server.rs avoids a separate file and matches the existing crate structure. It's re-exported from lib.rs for external use.

reuse.where: server.rs tests, lifecycle.rs, interactive_edit_loop.rs, bridge.rs, lib.rs
reuse.how: Adapt to LspService::new(|c| ReifyLanguageServer::with_sink(c, sink)) pattern. Create a test helper to reduce boilerplate across 10+ test call sites.
TauriNotificationSink follows the same app.emit(event_name, payload).ok() pattern used throughout main.rs for all Tauri events.

reuse.where: server.rs eval_state, main.rs AppState watcher field
reuse.how: RecordingSink uses Arc<Mutex<Vec<...>>> for test capture, same pattern as eval_state.

reuse.where: main.rs lsp_request command already uses json!({uri, diagnostics})
reuse.how: TauriNotificationSink uses the same json! pattern for the diagnostics event payload.

reuse.where: All existing server.rs and bridge tests
reuse.how: New RecordingSink tests reuse the same bracket_source() fixture for consistent test data.
Existing get_diagnostics() via ServerState.last_published_diagnostics still works as the internal capture mechanism, independent of sink routing.
